items.original_title: China lodges protest with Philippines after latest clash near Second Thomas Shoal
items.rss_summary:
China has lodged a formal protest with the Philippines, accusing it of initiating an attack on its coastguards following the latest clash between the two sides in the South China Sea.
Monday’s clash near the disputed Second Thomas Shoal involved a Chinese coastguard vessel and two Philippine Navy rubber dinghies.
The China Coast Guard said the Philippines had “illegally” launched the dinghies from the BRP Sierra Madre – an old warship that was intentionally grounded on the shoal in 1999 – then..

items.original_title: Fall in line or resign: Malaysia’s Anwar reins in coalition partners ahead of state poll
items.rss_summary:
Malaysia’s Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has threatened to remove ministers and federal appointees who use public office to attack their own government partners, drawing a new red line as the ruling coalition’s rival camps collide again ahead of the Negeri Sembilan state election on August 1.
Anwar said senior officials were free to campaign for their parties but must step down to avoid weaponising their official positions against allies in his unity government.

items.rss_summary: ❗️Индия вызвала временного поверенного в делах России Владимира Ладанова после гибели четырех индийских моряков при ударе по судну в Черном море, пишет Hindustan Times.О гибели индийских моряков стало известно 20 июля. Сухогруз MV Golden Leo выходил из украинского порта в Одессе. Его экипаж состоял из граждан Индии и Сирии. Ранее МИД Индии осудил атаку на судно, однако в его заявлении Россия не упоминалась.

items.rss_summary:
New findings on Chinese financing in the Philippines are challenging the assumption that Beijing reduced its funding under President Ferdinand Marcos Jnr because of worsening tensions in the South China Sea.
Instead, Chinese capital had continued to support Filipino businesses in recent years, including a US$3.9 billion syndicated loan to telecommunications company Dito Telecommunity in 2023, the largest ever China-backed private-sector loan to the Philippines, AidData, a US-based research...
